heading: A little about me.
paragraphs: I’m Sib, a graphic designer who likes making things look good and make sense.
I love food. I love traveling (love love love it). I care a lot about the people I work with, and I’m always trying to be a little better than I was the day before.
Hummingbird is where I keep my design work, branding projects, marketing bits, and the odd creative experiment.

faq.a: I help businesses communicate better through design. Branding, packaging, retail campaigns, presentations, social media, and pretty much anything that helps a brand connect with people.

faq.q: What do you enjoy working on the most?
faq.a: Branding projects and full campaigns where I get the creative freedom to build something from the ground up.

faq.q: How much experience do you have?
faq.a: 9+ years in Muscat, working with brands big and small.
Mostly Illustrator. I also use Photoshop, After Effects, Blender, and I’m currently learning DaVinci Resolve.

Mostly, yes. When a project calls for skills beyond mine, I collaborate with trusted Arabic copywriters, photographers, videographers, and other specialists. We come together as one team for your project and focus on getting the best result.

faq.q: Will you just do exactly what I ask?
faq.a: I’ll always listen first, understand what you’re trying to achieve, and then recommend what I genuinely believe is the best solution. The strongest projects happen when your knowledge of the business meets my experience in design.

faq.q: Are you serious about your work?
faq.a: Very. I take my work seriously. I just wanted this website to feel simple, approachable, and a little fun.

faq.q: Is this everything you’ve worked on?
faq.a: Not even close. A lot of my work is covered by NDAs, so some of my favourite projects can’t be shared publicly.

faq.q: What equipment and software do you use?
faq.a: An Apple ecosystem I’m emotionally and financially committed to. Adobe is a love hate relationship I can’t quit. Everything else, from Envato and Canva to Midjourney, Runway, ElevenLabs, and whatever comes next, joins the workflow whenever it makes sense.

faq.q: Will you make my logo bigger?
faq.a: Only if it actually needs to be bigger. 🙂

howSub: A bit like the bird, honestly.

traits.title: Tiny, but unforgettable.
traits.body: Good work doesn’t need to be loud.

traits.title: Curious by nature.
traits.body: I like learning new things instead of staying inside one box.

traits.title: Adaptable.
traits.body: Every project is different, and I enjoy switching between branding, design, and marketing.

traits.title: Colorful.
traits.body: Not because everything should be loud, but because good design should have personality.

traits.title: Independent.
traits.body: They don’t travel in flocks. Neither do I.

closing: Hummingbird isn’t pretending to be something bigger.
